privileged_info_summary: The food.gdt dataset is a real dataset from Principles of Econometrics, 5th Edition, containing 40 observations of weekly food expenditure (y) and weekly income in $100 units (x). The income ranges from 3.69 to 33.4 units ($369-$3340/week), with a mean of 19.60 units ($1960/week). When split at income > 20 as specified, it creates two groups of 20 observations each: High Income (reference group, mean income $2467/week, mean food exp $326.96) and Low Income (mean income $1454/week, mean food exp $240.18). The correct Oaxaca-Blinder decomposition using Group A as reference yields: Total Gap = 86.78, Explained Effect = 144.47, Unexplained Effect = -57.69. However, the setup script contains hardcoded fallback values (145.5, 105.2, 40.3) that do not match these calculated values, indicating either incorrect fallback values or a different computation method than described.
